mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization, which generates positive earnings surprises and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, elevated slack weakens consumer demand and weighs on corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Over a 5-year rolling window, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of month-over-month payroll expansion in PAYEMS and month-over-month decline in UNRATE signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts discretionary spending forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases simultaneously, the average 5-day SPX forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free rate and equity discount rates, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that translates into revenue beats for consumer-facing firms, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 with 20-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1.2 million units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, positive 60-day SPX forward returns occur more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that raises broad corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3% signals demand-pull inflation that erodes corporate margin forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When month-over-month CPIAUCSL change exceeds 0.3%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.
